title: rosa canina subvar. aciphylla
intro: Rosa canina subvar. aciphylla, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subvariety of the Rosa canina species, a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. aciphylla lie in its hips, which are rich in vitamins, flavonoids, and antioxidants, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against age-related diseases. Rosa canina subvar. aciphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, which can be used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
